title: Gymnopteris aurita
intro: Gymnopteris aurita, also known as the auriculated leather fern, is a species of fern in the family Osmundaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and fever, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Gymnopteris aurita include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions.
